story: In a quiet forest, a cruel boy lures girls beneath an ancient apple tree. He flatters, lays a picnic, and when she reaches for a bright apple he always teases—offering it, then snatching it back to take the first bite himself, the small ritual that precedes worse harm. One victim’s spirit will not rest. Through the winter she moves in the branches, shaking blooms until one fruit remains, and she pours all their grief and fury into that single perfect-looking apple so that inside it is ruin, a poison shaped by memory. Months pass. Summer returns. He arrives again with a new girl, spreads the blanket, makes her laugh, and together they notice the lone ripe apple hanging just within reach.
implausible_knowledge: she pours all their grief and fury into that single perfect-looking apple so that inside it is ruin, a poison shaped by memory
question: When he reaches for that lone apple during the picnic, what will he most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Hand the apple to the girl and insist she take the first bite instead.
answer_without_knowledge: Snatch the apple and take the first bite himself, as in his usual ritual.
